evals.prompt: I want to recreate https://linear.app — can you do a clone-study of it? Goal is a video showing how close we can get.
evals.expected_output: Capture runs, analysis identifies Next.js + Framer Motion + Tailwind stack, recreation brief lists all visible sections with animation specs, scaffolded project is built section by section, diff loop runs at least 2 iterations, teardown.md exists at the end.

evals.prompt: Study this site and rebuild it: https://www.lusion.co — I want it to look as close to the original as possible.
evals.expected_output: Capture runs and detects WebGL/Three.js. Analysis flags this as a high-difficulty bespoke-asset target. Recreation brief honestly states what can and cannot be recreated faithfully, substitutes assets per the playbook, and produces a teardown that explains where the gap is and why.

evals.prompt: Can you make my landing page look like https://stripe.com? Same kind of layered gradients, scroll behavior, the works.
evals.expected_output: Skill clarifies this is a study/recreation, not a pixel copy. Captures stripe.com, extracts the gradient/typography tokens, builds a recreation, but also generates substitute copy (not Stripe's verbatim) and uses a placeholder brand wordmark — never the Stripe logo.
